title: 帷幕
summary:
Captain Hastings recounts his habit of providing Hercule Poirot with detailed daily accounts to maintain their connection. During a conversation, Miss Creevy informs Hastings that Mrs. Lettrél has regained consciousness and wishes to see her husband. Hastings finds Lieutenant Colonel Lettrél in a morning room, appearing frightened and physically shaken. As Hastings assists the unsteady Colonel to the bedroom, the couple shares a tender, affectionate moment, which causes Hastings to feel ashamed of his previous suspicion that the Colonel had intentionally shot his wife.

At dinner, Hastings observes the guests: Mrs. Franklin appears well in a pink gown despite Dr. Franklin’s troubled demeanor; Alton and Judith depart for the garden; and Boyd Carrington chats with Mrs. Franklin. Elizabeth Cole remains engrossed in a book, appearing uncomfortable around Hastings. Later, Hastings finds Colonel Lettrél in Poirot's room, where the Colonel nostalgically recounts falling in love with his wife at a dance.

Hastings discusses the shooting with Poirot, admitting that both he and Carrington had suspected the event was intentional. Poirot remains reserved, noting that genuine affection can exist behind animosity and that things are not always as they seem. As Hastings prepares for sleep, he develops a new theory: a third party, "X," might have fired a second shot simultaneously with the Colonel's shot at a rabbit to mask a murder as an accident. He concludes that if the Colonel confesses to an accidental shooting, the police might not perform the ballistic tests necessary to reveal the involvement of a second weapon.
